old_claim.note: Advanced Chemistry is a plausible dependency for a forecast technology and should be treated as speculative.

new_claim.note: The original Ba-La-Cu-O work depended on prepared mixed oxide ceramic compositions and processing, which is chemistry/materials synthesis evidence rather than a generic forecast edge.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract describes prepared Ba-La-Cu-O compounds in polycrystalline form and superconducting onset behavior.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The first high-Tc report involved synthesized mixed oxide compositions.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The primary paper supports chemistry/material synthesis as enabling the material discovery.

ontology_before: The graph treated chemistry as a speculative future dependency.
ontology_after: The graph treats chemistry as enabling synthesis for the established high-Tc material class.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to theory-only high-Tc superconductivity.
A more specific ceramic oxide synthesis node is added and this edge is rewired.
short_reason: The first high-Tc materials were synthesized mixed oxides.
